summary: Reference context structure is sound but contains critical spec path validation error (missing 'cclab-' prefix) and 6 unresolved '?' spec entries. Existing related specs are correctly identified but not explicitly cross-referenced in the table.
findings: Reference context table uses '?' for all 6 spec entries instead of actual paths from .score/tech_design/
main_spec_ref format is invalid: 'crates/sdd/logic/structured-issue.md' is missing 'cclab-' prefix and should be 'crates/cclab-sdd/logic/structured-issue.md'

Existing related specs correctly identified (state-machine.md, restructure-input.md, pre-clarifications.md, reference-context.md, post-clarifications.md, issues-backend.md) but not explicitly referenced in the table
Missing explicit spec reference for init_change routing logic — should cross-reference dispatch-model.md or create new init-change-spec.md
Issue section parser design (YAML frontmatter extension, structured markdown detection) not addressed in any referenced spec
Artifact output formats (requirements.md, pre_clarifications.md with 'status: answered') mentioned in pre-clarifications but not centrally documented
CLI subcommand interface for 'score issues enrich' mentioned but not spec'd — needs sdd-cli.md update or new spec
issues: CRITICAL: main_spec_ref missing 'cclab-' crate name prefix
HIGH: Reference context table rows 1-6 show '?' instead of actual spec paths — makes requirements unverifiable
HIGH: No spec path for init_change structured issue detection and phase skip routing logic
MEDIUM: Issue section parser design not documented in any existing spec
MEDIUM: 'score issues enrich' subcommand interface not formally spec'd

checklist.notes: Spec plan sections (overview, schema, cli, changes) are appropriate for a feature that spans issue format, parsing logic, artifact generation, and CLI integration. However, the main_spec_ref path format violation prevents approval.
